title: Please update the qemu-user-static version in podman machine
body:
### Issue Description

The qemu-user package got an update recently to fix bug https://github.com/osbuild/bootc-image-builder/issues/207 - the issue is a missing ioctl() (FIFREEZE/FITHAW). The patch is on the upstream ML is in https://lists.nongnu.org/archive/html/qemu-devel/2024-02/msg03971.html 

It got released into fedora recently too: https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2024-90617acee3

Please update the podman machien image to include " qemu-8.1.3-4.fc39 " so that users get the fix.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. run `sudo podman run --rm -it --privileged --pull=newer --security-opt label=type:unconfined_t quay.io/centos-bootc/bootc-image-builder:lates  --type raw http://quay.io/rhel-edge/centos-bootc-test:v7vu`


### Describe the results you received

It fails with:
```
error: Performing deployment: Full sync: During fsfreeze-thaw: ioctl(FIFREEZE): Inappropriate ioctl for device`
``

### Describe the results you expected

It does not fail

### podman info output

```yaml
-
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: you dont provide version information which is asked for in the template ... are you using podman 4 or podman 5-dev or what

@rhatdan @baude 

@benoitf and I are on the latest 5.0-dev, and on the current machine it does not have the updated qemu with the fix:

```
$ rpm -qa | grep qemu
qemu-user-static-x86-8.1.3-2.fc39.aarch64
```

We would need `8.1.3-4`
ok, i'll build a one off image for you and we can test
I have added to our base containerfile -> https://github.com/baude/podman-machine-images/blob/main/podman-image-daily/Containerfile#L43

Using the test image from here:

https://fedorapeople.org/groups/podman/testing/podman-machine-daily.aarch64.applehv.raw.zst

I am able to confirm that it's now in the podman machine:
```
root@localhost:~# rpm -qa | grep qemu
qemu-user-static-x86-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64
qemu-user-static-xtensa-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64

qemu-user-static-hppa-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64
qemu-user-static-hexagon-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64
qemu-user-static-cris-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64
qemu-user-static-arm-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64
qemu-user-static-alpha-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64
qemu-user-static-aarch64-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64
qemu-user-static-8.1.3-4.fc39.aarch64
```

I'm now able to use https://github.com/osbuild/bootc-image-builder to build a amd64 image on a mac M1 podman machine (using latest 5.0 dev), specifically an amd64 qcow2 image:

```
